creator: lapo@lapo.it
text:
WARNING: I've been told on the official Victoria Metrics Telegram channel that LTS releases only make sense in the private enterprise repository (which is not opensource) and the tags on github.com are nothing to do with that, they are only (misleading) tags done at the same time in order to have binaries availables on github.

https://docs.victoriametrics.com/lts-releases/

Actually this can be seen in the git history, as all the tags for the various LTS active branches are done on the same master branch release:

bc69d5f has both tags v1.110.2 and 1.102.14
6ed31b9 has both tags v1.110.1 and 1.102.13
and so on.

So, really, the versions we're releasing on FreeBSD Ports are not "LTS releases", they are not really releases at all in fact (they are a random point on the master branch, so stricly less stable than any real release).

We should immediately switch to latest release. (which is a real release)
